alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy of the developed models was validated through 10-fold cross-validation.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a methodological validation detail (10-fold cross-validation) that was not present in the preprint claims, so this is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our ResNet can predict correct folds (TMscore>0.5) for 26 out of 32 CASP13 FM targets with precision over 80%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A large ResNet can predict structures of correct folds for 26 out of 32 CASP13 free-modelling targets and L/5 long-range contacts with precision over 80%. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When co-evolution is not used, our ResNet can still predict correct folds for 18 of the 32 CASP13 FM targets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: When co-evolution is not used, ResNet can still predict structures of correct folds for 18 CASP13 free-modelling targets, greatly exceeding previous methods that do not use co-evolution. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: With only primary sequence, our ResNet can predict correct folds for all 21 human-designed proteins we tested. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Even with only the primary sequence, ResNet can predict the structures of correct folds for all tested human-designed proteins. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ResNet may fare better for the human-designed proteins when trained without co-evolution information than with co-evolution. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ResNet may fare better for the designed proteins when trained without co-evolution than with co-evolution. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These results suggest that ResNet does not simply denoise co-evolution signals, but instead is able to learn important sequence-structure relationships from experimental structures. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepTAP is a tool for TAP-binding peptide prediction that employs a sequence-based multilayered recurrent neural network. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TargetP 2.0 is a novel state of art method to identify N-terminal sorting signals that direct proteins to the secretory pathway, mitochondria, and chloroplasts or other plastids. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two-thirds of chloroplast and thylakoid transit peptides have an alanine in position two, while only 20% of other plant proteins do. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Less than 30% of targeting peptides in fungi and single-celled eukaryotes have an amino acid that allows the removal of the N-terminal methionine, compared with 60% for proteins without targeting peptide. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Less than 30% of targeting peptides in fungi and single-celled eukaryotes allow the removal of the N-terminal methionine, compared to 60% for proteins without targeting peptide. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same quantitative result with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepMicro significantly reduces the dimensionality of the marker profile, accelerating the model training and hyperparameter optimization procedure with 8X–30X speedup over the basic approach. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The implementation of transfer learning in BioStructNet optimizes prediction accuracy for small data sets. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BioStructNet would accelerate the discovery of functional enzymes for industrial use, especially when training datasets for machine learning are small. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BioStructNet would accelerate the discovery of functional enzymes for industrial use, particularly in cases where the training data sets for machine learning are small. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Transformer model trained on covert speech achieved an average token error rate (TER) of 46.6% for decoding covert speech. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Transformer model trained on covert speech achieved an average token error rate (TER) of 46.6% for decoding covert speech. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model trained on overt speech achieved a TER of 46.3% for decoding covert speech. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model trained on overt speech achieved a TER of 46.3% for decoding covert speech. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model can be used to better delineate spatial domains of biological interest. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Array Melt measures equilibrium stability of millions of DNA hairpins simultaneously using fluorescence-based quenching signals. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BAI-Net outperforms the conventional iterative clustering approach by capturing significantly heritable topographic variations in individualized cartographies. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using the proposed approach, we are able to identify brain-based measures that simultaneously encode the exposure variable and correlate with the behavioral outcome. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The IMage Perturbation Autoencoder (IMPA) predicts cellular morphological effects of chemical and genetic perturbations using untreated cells as input. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: IMage Perturbation Autoencoder (IMPA) accurately captures morphological and population-level changes of both seen and unseen perturbations on breast cancer and osteosarcoma cells. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our analysis revealed subtle, yet significant differences in intracellular behavior between IAsp 655 and HI 655. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study highlights the potential of our approach to decipher subtle differences in intracellular trafficking and sorting characteristics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: WaveNet outperformed the autoregressive model in estimating the signal amplitude and phase. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: WaveNet outperformed the autoregressive model in estimating the signal amplitude and phase. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance comparison with identical framing and certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The probabilistic approach offers a way of forecasting even more accurately while effectively discarding uncertain predictions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Probabilistic deep learning can be used to forecast resting-state EEG time series. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed model can enhance the real-time estimation of brain states in brain-computer interfaces and brain stimulation protocols. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Virtual reality annotation significantly accelerated training data generation, allowing DELiVR to outperform state-of-the-art cell-segmenting approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning models can infer cancer patient prognosis from molecular and anatomic pathology information. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication presents a more specific, quantitative performance claim against clinical baselines rather than the general statement that models can infer prognosis, so the general claim is revised to a specific performance result.

alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same mathematical equivalence and parallelizability claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Empirical analysis demonstrates an order-of-magnitude speed up using modern GPUs and CPUs with the pNeRF algorithm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Empirical analysis demonstrates an order-of-magnitude speed up using modern GPUs and CPUs with the pNeRF algorithm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The empirical speed-up result is retained unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Switching to pNeRF can reduce the fractional computational cost of coordinate conversion from over two-thirds to around 10% in machine learning-based workflows. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Switching to pNeRF can reduce the fractional computational cost of coordinate conversion from over two-thirds to around 10% in machine learning-based workflows. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An optimized TensorFlow-based implementation of pNeRF is available on GitHub. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication omits mention of the TensorFlow implementation on GitHub that the preprint reported.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A classifier based on K-median or K-mean-clustering followed by Artificial Neural Networks is a highly reliable and accurate tool to predict if a single rat is vulnerable or resilient to addiction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classifier developed using K-median or K-mean-clustering followed by artificial neural networks is a highly reliable and accurate tool for predicting addiction vulnerability in rats. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the method and the high-accuracy predictive claim for rats using the same clustering + ANN pipeline.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine-learning-based classifier objectively labels single individuals as resilient or vulnerable to develop addiction-like behaviour in multisymptomatic preclinical models of cocaine or alcohol addiction-like behaviour in rats. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine-learning-based classifier correctly labels rats as resilient or vulnerable based on their addiction-like behavior. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the classifier's objective labeling of individual rats' resilience/vulnerability based on addiction-like behavior.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This novel dimension-based classifier increases the heuristic value and generalizability of preclinical models while providing proof of principle for the deployment of similar tools for the future diagnosis of psychiatric disorders. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This novel dimension-based classifier enhances the heuristic value of preclinical models of addiction and supports the development of similar diagnostic tools for psychiatric disorders. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core conclusion about enhanced heuristic value and potential diagnostic tool development but slightly rephrases and omits explicit wording about "generalizability" and "proof of principle."

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CellSium is a flexibly configurable cell simulator designed to synthesize realistic image sequences of bacterial microcolonies growing in monolayers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CellSium is a flexibly configurable cell simulator designed to synthesize realistic image sequences of bacterial microcolonies growing in monolayers.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim verbatim with the same framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The simulated images produced by CellSium are suitable for training neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The simulated images produced by CellSium are suitable for training neural networks.
alignments.rationale: Claim content and strength are preserved between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CellSium supports the generation of synthetic time-lapse videos with and without fluorescence, using programmable cell growth models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CellSium supports the generation of synthetic time-lapse videos with and without fluorescence, using programmable cell growth models.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same contribution description and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CellSium includes simulation-ready 3D colony geometries for computational fluid dynamics (CFD).
alignments.pub_claim_text: CellSium includes simulation-ready 3D colony geometries for computational fluid dynamics.
alignments.rationale: The feature is carried over unchanged into the published paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DeepET model outperforms classical regression models in predicting enzyme optimal catalytic temperatures and protein melting temperatures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DeepET model outperforms classical regression models in predicting enzyme optimal catalytic temperatures and protein melting temperatures.
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is preserved verbatim with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The protein-temperature representations learned by DeepET provide a temperature-related statistical summary of protein sequences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The protein-temperature representations learned by DeepET provide a temperature-related statistical summary of protein sequences.
alignments.rationale: The claim content is the same but the publication presents it with lower stated certainty/framing (medium vs high).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepET holds promise for understanding enzyme thermal adaptation and guiding the engineering of thermostable enzymes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepET holds promise for understanding enzyme thermal adaptation and guiding the engineering of thermostable enzymes.
alignments.rationale: The concluding/forward-looking claim is retained with similar wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A robust and generalizable CNN-based system for automatically detecting and counting whales from space has been proposed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A CNN-based system for automatically detecting and counting whales in satellite and aerial images was developed.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the system but clarifies the input data as "satellite and aerial images," broadening the original "from space" phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system achieved a performance (F1-measure) of 84% in detecting and 97% in counting 80 whales.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system achieved an F1-measure performance of 81% in detecting and 94% in counting whales.
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is present but the reported metrics were updated (revised downward) in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Applying this cost-effective method worldwide could facilitate the assessment of whale populations to guide conservation actions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Applying this cost-effective method worldwide could contribute to the assessment of whale populations to guide conservation actions.
alignments.rationale: The conservation-applicability conclusion is retained with only minor wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Free and global access to high-resolution imagery for conservation purposes would boost the process of assessing whale populations.
alignments.rationale: This specific advocacy for free/global imagery access does not appear in the published claims and was omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining the two steps of the whale counting approach increased accuracy by 36% compared to a baseline detection model alone.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a new quantitative claim about combining steps improving accuracy that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Residual Deep Neural Network showed high accuracy (95%) when distinguishing periods of clean, artefact-free EEG from any kind of artefact.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Residual Deep Neural Network showed high accuracy (95%) when distinguishing periods of clean, artefact-free EEG from any kind of artefact.
alignments.rationale: The main accuracy result is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy in identifying the five different types of artefacts ranged from 57%-92%, with electrode pop being the hardest to detect and EMG being the easiest.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy in identifying the five different types of artefacts ranged from 57%-92%, with electrode pop being the hardest to detect and EMG being the easiest.
alignments.rationale: The claim is the same but the publication raises the stated certainty to high; content unchanged but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EEG channels located near the vertex were the least susceptible to artefact.
alignments.pub_claim_text: EEG channels located near the vertex were the least susceptible to artefact.
alignments.rationale: This channel-susceptibility observation is preserved in the publication with similar framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Artefacts can be accurately and reliably identified in the neonatal EEG using a deep learning algorithm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Artefacts can be accurately and reliably identified in the neonatal EEG using a deep learning algorithm.
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim about reliable artefact identification is unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The detection accuracy was lower on the validation set (87%).
alignments.rationale: The publication reports an additional validation-set accuracy result that does not appear in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Elite soccer players demonstrate heightened planning and memory capacities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Elite soccer players demonstrate heightened planning and memory capacities.
alignments.rationale: The result is carried forward verbatim into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Elite soccer players exhibit enhanced executive functions, especially cognitive flexibility.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Elite soccer players exhibit enhanced executive functions, especially cognitive flexibility.
alignments.rationale: The executive-function finding is preserved without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Elite soccer players possess elevated levels of conscientiousness, extraversion, and openness to experience, coupled with reduced neuroticism and agreeableness.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Elite soccer players have elevated levels of conscientiousness, extraversion, and openness to experience, coupled with reduced neuroticism and agreeableness.
alignments.rationale: Personality trait findings remain substantively identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research provides insights into the psychology of elite soccer players, holding significance for talent identification and development strategies in soccer.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research provides insights into the psychology of elite soccer players, holding significance for talent identification and development strategies in soccer.
alignments.rationale: The concluding implication is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new method for predicting chemical toxicity based on molecular structure demonstrates high predictive performance for algae, aquatic invertebrates, and fish. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An AI-based model using transformers and deep neural networks can predict chemical toxicity with high predictive performance for algae, aquatic invertebrates, and fish. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the high predictive-performance result but explicitly identifies the model as transformer/deep-neural-network based, adding methodological detail without changing the claim's substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model has a larger applicability domain and considerably lower error compared to commonly used QSAR methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI-based model has a larger applicability domain and considerably lower error compared to commonly used QSAR methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same comparative-performance claim with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Training the model on data that combines multiple types of effect concentrations further improves its performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Training the model on data with multiple effect concentrations (EC50/EC10) further improves its performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same improvement result but specifies the effect-concentration types (EC50/EC10), clarifying the data used.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Transformer-based models have the potential to significantly advance computational predictions of chemical toxicity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning and transformers have the potential to markedly advance computational prediction of chemical toxicity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the claim to include deep learning more generally (broader scope) and states it with higher certainty, increasing confidence compared with the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Confidence typically correlates with neural activity positively in a ventromedial-prefrontal (VMPFC) network and negatively in a dorsolateral and dorsomedial prefrontal network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Confidence estimates correlate positively with neural activity in a ventromedial-prefrontal (VMPFC) network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the positive VMPFC correlation but separates/omits the negative dorsal-prefrontal part into a distinct claim, effectively narrowing this specific claim's scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dorsal prefrontal network correlates negatively with a condition-specific confidence signal. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dorsal prefrontal network correlates negatively with a condition-specific confidence signal. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this negative correlation finding with no substantive change in scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Decision-related VMPFC activity better correlates with confidence than with option-values inferred from reinforcement-learning models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Decision-related VMPFC activity better correlates with confidence than with option-values inferred from reinforcement-learning models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward this comparative neural-correlation result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The VMPFC is identified as a key node in the neuro-computational architecture that builds global feeling-of-confidence signals from latent decision variables and contextual biases during reinforcement-learning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The VMPFC is identified as a key node in the neuro-computational architecture that builds global feeling-of-confidence signals. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core conclusion about VMPFC as a key node but omits explicit mechanistic detail about latent decision variables and contextual biases, narrowing the claim's expressed scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The pupil closely tracks reinforcement learning both across trials and participants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pupil closely tracks reinforcement learning processes independently across participants and across trials. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding that pupil dynamics track reinforcement-learning across trials and participants with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Prior to choice, the pupil dilated as a function of trial-by-trial fluctuations in value beliefs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Prior to choice, the pupil dilated as a function of trial-by-trial fluctuations in value beliefs about the to-be chosen option. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies that pre-choice dilation tracked value beliefs about the to-be-chosen option, a more specific instantiation of the preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: After feedback, early dilation scaled with value uncertainty, whereas later constriction scaled with reward prediction errors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: After feedback, a biphasic pupil response was observed, the amplitude of which correlated with participants' learning rates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a biphasic amplitude–learning-rate relationship rather than the specific early-uncertainty / later-RPE mapping described in the preprint, indicating a substantive revision of the feedback-timecourse interpretation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computational approach systematically implicates the pupil in value-based decisions and the processing of violated value beliefs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pupil size fluctuations can provide detailed information about the computations underlying value-based decisions and the subsequent updating of value beliefs. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the conclusion implicating the pupil in value-based decision computations and updating, while stating it with higher certainty and more explicit wording.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Pupillometry can be used as an accessible tool to non-invasively study the processes underlying ongoing reinforcement learning in the clinic. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The clinical-applicability claim appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint; it extends the work's implications toward clinical use.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed solution combines citizen science and machine learning to enhance the labeling of large datasets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining citizen science and deep learning can generalize and scale expert decision making. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same contribution of combining citizen science and learning methods to scale expert decisions, framing it as generalizable and scalable.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning algorithm trained on citizen scientist labels performed as well as a state-of-the-art algorithm for quality control of MRI images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning performed as well as specialized algorithms for quality control with an AUC of 0.99. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the performance-as-good result and provides a numeric AUC, clarifying the performance metric without changing the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method allows for the amplification of expert labels through citizen scientists, enabling the analysis of larger datasets. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A deep learning algorithm was trained to predict data quality based on citizen scientist labels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication concretely describes the mechanism (training a model on citizen-scientist labels) that enables amplification, and presents it with higher stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates a relationship between brain image quality and the replicability of the relationship between brain volume and age. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim explicitly restates the reported relationship between image quality and replicability of the brain-volume/age effect, so this preprint-specific result appears omitted from the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Large Language Models (LLMs) are beneficial in predicting neural signals related to narrative processing. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LLMs predict brain activity effectively only when using short contextual windows of up to a few dozen words. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication constrains LLM utility to short-context windows and states it with higher confidence, altering the preprint's broader, lower-certainty benefit claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The incoming context to the brain is limited to short windows of a few tens of words. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The brain integrates short-term and long-term contextual information through an incremental mechanism, unlike LLMs that process large text windows in parallel. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a different view: the brain integrates short- and long-term context incrementally, which revises the preprint's assertion that incoming context is limited to short windows.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural activities at the default-mode network (DMN) were better predicted by the incremental model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An alternative LLM-based incremental-context model significantly enhances the prediction of neural activity in higher-order regions involved in long-timescale processing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication aligns with the preprint's DMN / higher-order-region finding but frames it as an LLM-based incremental-context model improving prediction for long-timescale regions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lower-level brain areas were better predicted with short-context-window LLM. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LLMs predict brain activity effectively only when using short contextual windows of up to a few dozen words. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes that short-context windows are required for effective LLM prediction of brain activity, which clarifies the preprint's observation about lower-level areas.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed adaptive closed-loop control framework successfully accelerates the wound healing process by 17.71%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report any numeric acceleration or an explicit claim that the framework speeds wound healing by a specific percentage; the acceleration result from the preprint is absent in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The data-driven model estimates a 40% acceleration in wound closure. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim reports a 40% acceleration or similar quantitative wound-closure estimate, so this preprint result was not carried into the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach eliminates the need for mathematical modeling of complex nonlinear wound healing dynamics. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepMapper is an unsupervised deep learning approach that can learn a linear representation of the complex, nonlinear wound healing dynamics from a time series of wound images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution as an unsupervised method that learns representations from data rather than the stronger preprint claim of outright eliminating mathematical modelling; this is a clarification and a tempered, narrower formulation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Real-time monitoring allows for precise adjustment in treatment strategies that align with an individual’s unique biological response. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The paper does not assert real-time treatment adjustment or personalization based on monitoring in its listed claims, so this specific conclusion does not appear in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Arousal was increased during exposure of erotic vs. neutral cues both on the subjective and autonomic level. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Arousal was increased during exposure of erotic versus neutral cues both on the subjective and autonomic level. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result wording and scope about subjective and autonomic arousal increases.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Erotic cue exposure nominally increased discounting as reflected by reduced choices of delayed options. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Erotic cue exposure increased discounting as reflected by more impatient choices. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published wording states the discounting effect more categorically (more impatient choices) compared with the preprint's "nominal" phrasing, representing a clarified and stronger presentation of the same finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model-based control during reinforcement learning was reduced following erotic cues according to model-agnostic analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model-based control during reinforcement learning was reduced following erotic cues according to model-agnostic analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is reproduced verbatim in the publication with the same analytic framing and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings replicate previous work on cue-reactivity effects in temporal discounting and for the first time show similar effects in model-based reinforcement learning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our findings replicate previous work on cue-reactivity effects in temporal discounting and for the first time show similar effects in model-based reinforcement learning in a heterosexual male sample. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the sample descriptor (heterosexual male), narrowing the generality of the original replication/novelty claim while keeping the substantive conclusion intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients with first-episode psychosis (FEP) showed significant impairments in overriding Pavlovian conflict, a lower learning rate, and a lower sensitivity to both reward and punishment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patients with first-episode psychosis (FEP) showed significant impairments in overriding Pavlovian conflict, a lower learning rate, and a lower sensitivity to both reward and punishment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this specific empirical result verbatim, with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Less widespread deficits in reinforcement learning were observed in individuals at risk for mental state (ARMS). (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Less widespread deficits in reinforcement learning were observed in individuals at risk for mental state (ARMS). (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This ARMS-related result appears unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Polygenic risk scores for schizophrenia did not significantly predict performance on the reinforcement learning task in the general population. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Polygenic risk scores for schizophrenia did not significantly predict performance on the reinforcement learning task in the general population. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this null genetic-association result with equivalent wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study does not support the role of reinforcement learning as an intermediate phenotype in psychosis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study does not support the role of reinforcement learning as an intermediate phenotype in psychosis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The overarching conclusion about reinforcement learning as an intermediate phenotype is preserved identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed deep-learning model predicts the potential allergenicity of proteins based on their primary structure represented as protein sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed enhanced deep learning model predicts the potential allergenicity of proteins based on their primary structure represented as protein sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same core claim about sequence-based allergenicity prediction, adding the adjective "enhanced" but not changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model classifies proteins into allergenic or non-allergenic classes with high sensitivity and specificity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model achieves a sensitivity of 97.91%, a specificity of 97.69%, an accuracy of 97.80%, and an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 99%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides the explicit performance metrics that substantiate the preprint's general claim about high sensitivity and specificity, clarifying the result with concrete numbers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model achieves a sensitivity of 97.91%, specificity of 97.69%, accuracy of 97.80%, and an area under the ROC curve of 99%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model achieves a sensitivity of 97.91%, a specificity of 97.69%, an accuracy of 97.80%, and an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 99%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact performance metrics, so this quantitative result is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of ensemble modeling techniques balances the model’s sensitivity and specificity and improves the outcome. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining feature vectors extracted from two protein language models improves the prediction results of protein allergenicity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the method used (combining feature vectors from two language models) and presents it as an improvement; this clarifies and strengthens the preprint's ensemble-related conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CRISPRclassify-CNN-Att demonstrates excellent performance in classifying multiple subtypes of CRISPR-Cas systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CRISPRclassify-CNN-Att demonstrates outstanding performance in classifying multiple subtypes of CRISPR loci. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same claim of strong multi-subtype classification performance with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CRISPRclassify-CNN-Att offers a new approach as a significant complement to current methods of CRISPR loci classification. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CRISPRclassify-CNN-Att offers a novel approach that serves as a significant complement to cas-based methods. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the same contribution as a novel complement to cas-based methods, consistent with the preprint's claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CRISPRclassify-CNN-Att can identify unclassified loci missed by traditional cas-based methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method enables the classification of orphan or distant CRISPR loci. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's wording about classifying orphan/distant loci conveys the same capability as identifying unclassified loci missed by cas-based methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method improves classification accuracy for subtypes with fewer samples through transfer learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not mention transfer learning or specific improvement for low-sample subtypes, so this transfer-learning-specific claim from the preprint is not reflected in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel attack algorithm can reconstruct privacy-protected data using basic functionalities available in established FL frameworks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduced attack algorithm can reconstruct privacy-protected data using basic functionalities available in several established FL frameworks. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The attack algorithm is robust to defense strategies that involve adding random noise. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The attack algorithm is robust to defense strategies that involve adding random noise. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim is verbatim between versions and retains the same assertion and confidence about robustness to noise defenses.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing frameworks have limitations that can be addressed by analyzing the implications of using differential privacy. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing frameworks have limitations that can be addressed by analyzing the implications of using differential privacy. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
