alignments.preprint_claim_text: The insights presented in this study will aid in the improvement of FL frameworks. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel insights presented in this study will aid in the improvement of FL frameworks. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication adds the adjective "novel," slightly clarifying the nature of the insights but preserving the same contribution claim and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: End-to-end training of a feature extractor using deep reinforcement learning agents can optimize stimulation patterns for visual neuroprostheses. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: End-to-end training of a feature extractor using deep reinforcement learning agents can optimize stimulation patterns for visual neuroprostheses. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Claim is the same in preprint and publication describing the end-to-end RL optimization for stimulation patterns.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Stimulation patterns obtained via task-dependent end-to-end optimized reinforcement learning result in equivalent or improved performance compared to fixed feature extractors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Stimulation patterns obtained via task-dependent end-to-end optimized reinforcement learning result in equivalent or improved performance compared to fixed feature extractors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical performance claim preserved between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed task-oriented evaluation framework allows for comparison of different stimulus generation mechanisms in visual neuroprostheses. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed task-oriented evaluation framework allows for comparison of different stimulus generation mechanisms in visual neuroprostheses. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution describing the evaluation framework is unchanged in content and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adaptive reinforcement learning is relevant for neuroprosthetic vision in complex environments. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adaptive reinforcement learning is relevant for neuroprosthetic vision in complex environments. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion about the relevance of adaptive RL is identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our study proposes a novel topic modeling approach for clustering single cells and detecting topics in single-cell datasets that measure multiple omics simultaneously. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our study proposes a novel topic modeling approach for clustering single cells and detecting topics in single-cell datasets that measure multiple omics simultaneously. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Contribution statement is the same in both versions describing the novel topic modeling approach for multi-omics single-cell data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified protein-coding genes and long noncoding RNAs (lncRNAs) that group thousands of cells into biologically similar clusters, accurately distinguishing drug-sensitive and resistant breast cancer types. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identified protein-coding genes and long non-coding RNAs (lncRNAs) that group thousands of cells into biologically similar clusters, accurately distinguishing drug-sensitive and -resistant breast cancer types. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication wording adjusts hyphenation and adds a clearer formatting of "drug-sensitive and -resistant," but the substantive result and confidence remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach offers optimal partitioning of genes into topics and cells into clusters simultaneously, producing easily interpretable clustering outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach offers an optimal partitioning of genes into topics and cells into clusters simultaneously, producing easily interpretable clustering outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion about simultaneous partitioning and interpretability is preserved unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An integrative clustering approach, which combines the information from mRNAs and lncRNAs treated as disjoint omics layers, enhances the accuracy of cell classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An integrative clustering approach, which combines the information from mRNAs and lncRNAs treated as disjoint omics layers, enhances the accuracy of cell classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result about integrative clustering improving classification accuracy is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Transfer learning using a pre-trained feature space required substantially less experimental time to achieve the same performance as data-driven approaches in predicting spiking activity in V1. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Transfer learning using a pre-trained feature space required substantially less experimental time to achieve the same performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication shortens wording and specifies awake monkeys elsewhere, but the core result about reduced experimental time for equivalent performance is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multi-layer convolutional neural networks (CNNs) set the new state of the art for predicting neural responses to natural images in primate V1. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multi-layer convolutional neural networks (CNNs) set the new state of the art for predicting neural responses to natural images in primate V1. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion about CNNs being state of the art is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep features learned for object recognition are better explanations for V1 computation than all previous filter bank theories. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep features learned for object recognition are better explanations for V1 computation than all previous filter bank theories. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim about deep features outperforming filter bank theories is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The transfer learning approach performed similarly well to the data-driven approach and both outperformed classical linear-nonlinear and wavelet-based feature representations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The transfer learning approach performed similarly well to the data-driven approach in predicting spiking activity in V1 of awake monkeys. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication separates and clarifies the transfer-vs-data-driven equivalence and specifies the experimental context (awake monkeys); the part about outperforming classical representations is present in a separate publication claim (Q1).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: V1 should be modeled as a result of several nonlinear stages, reflecting its complexity in processing visual information. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both deep learning approaches outperformed classical linear-nonlinear and wavelet-based feature representations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No direct publication equivalent for the conceptual modeling recommendation; the publication emphasizes comparative performance results rather than the specific modeling prescription, so mapping is weak and represents a shift from conceptual conclusion to concrete performance claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A convolutional neural network was developed to quantify nuclear localisation from fluorescence and bright-field images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We developed a convolutional neural network that determines nuclear localization from fluorescence and bright-field images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication rephrases development claim in active voice and American spelling, clarifying wording but keeping the same contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network based approach outperformed seven published methods in predicting single-cell time series. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural network-based approach outperformed seven published methods, particularly in predicting single-cell time series. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication adds nuance that the advantage is particular for time-series prediction, slightly clarifying the performance claim without changing its substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using machine learning to automatically determine the appropriate image processing consistently outperforms ad hoc methods. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using machine learning to automatically determine image processing consistently outperforms ad hoc approaches. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion about ML-based automated image processing outperforming ad hoc methods is preserved with only minor wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adopting machine learning methods promises to improve the accuracy and consistency of single-cell analyses. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adopting machine learning methods promises to improve the accuracy and consistency of single-cell analyses. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The broader conclusion about benefits of adopting ML methods is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: expiMap is a deep learning model that enables interpretable reference mapping using biologically understandable entities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ExpiMap is a biologically informed deep-learning architecture that enables single-cell reference mapping.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert the same core contribution: a biologically informed deep-learning architecture for interpretable reference mapping; wording is slightly different but meaning is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model retains similar integration performance as existing methods while providing a biologically interpretable framework for understanding cellular behavior.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ExpiMap compares favourably to existing methods while bringing an additional layer of interpretability to integrative single-cell analysis.
alignments.rationale: Both claims claim comparable performance to existing methods plus an added interpretability layer; publication wording restates the same result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The interpretable nature of the mapping revealed unreported associations between interferon signaling via the RIG-I/MDA5 and GPCRs pathways in severe COVID-19.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ExpiMap resolves responses of patients who have coronavirus disease 2019 to different treatments across cell types.
alignments.rationale: The preprint reports a specific novel molecular association in severe COVID-19; the publication makes a more general claim about resolving patient responses to treatments, which is a changed and broader claim with lower stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: expiMap enabled the direct comparison of a diverse set of pancreatic beta cells from multiple studies, revealing a strong correlation between the unfolded protein response and asparagine N-linked glycosylation.
alignments.rationale: The specific pancreatic beta-cell correlation described in the preprint does not appear in the publication's listed claims and therefore is treated as removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Hair Cell Analysis Toolbox (HCAT) automates common image analysis tasks related to auditory hair cells.
alignments.pub_claim_text: HCAT automates common image analysis tasks such as counting hair cells, classifying them by subtype, determining their best frequency, and generating cochleograms.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides concrete examples of the automated tasks described in the preprint, clarifying the claim rather than changing its meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HCAT allows for faster, unbiased, and more comprehensive data analysis practices in cochlear image analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The automated tools provided by HCAT remove a considerable barrier in cochlear image analysis, allowing for faster, unbiased, and more comprehensive data analysis practices.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication make the same conclusion about enabling faster, unbiased, and more comprehensive analysis; language is consistent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HCAT can be trained to develop custom deep learning detection models for various biological tissues.
alignments.pub_claim_text: HCAT's core codebase can be trained to develop a custom deep learning detection model for any object on an image with some training data.
alignments.rationale: The publication broadens the training capability from 'various biological tissues' to 'any object on an image,' which changes the scope of the original preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MorphoGenie is an unsupervised deep-learning framework that addresses challenges in single-cell morphological profiling.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MorphoGenie is an unsupervised deep-learning framework for single-cell morphological profiling.
alignments.rationale: Both descriptions state the same contribution: an unsupervised deep-learning framework for single-cell morphological profiling.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MorphoGenie enables the extraction of biologically meaningful features without human annotation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MorphoGenie creates a compact, interpretable latent space that captures biologically meaningful features without annotation.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint's result with slightly more detail about the latent space but preserves the original meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MorphoGenie introduces a systematic approach to mapping disentangled latent representations to fundamental hierarchical morphological attributes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MorphoGenie systematically links latent representations to hierarchical morphological attributes, ensuring semantic and biological interpretability.
alignments.rationale: Both claims describe the same methodological contribution linking latent factors to hierarchical morphological attributes; publication language emphasizes interpretability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework enhances the model’s capacity to generalize across a broad spectrum of imaging modalities and experimental conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MorphoGenie enables robust performance across diverse imaging modalities and experimental conditions.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the generalization claim as robust performance across modalities and conditions, matching the preprint's intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MorphoGenie offers a new, generalized strategy for unbiased and comprehensive morphological profiling.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MorphoGenie provides a generalized, unbiased strategy for morphological profiling, revealing cellular behaviors often overlooked by expert visual examination.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the claim by noting specific benefits (revealing overlooked behaviors) but states it with somewhat lower certainty; this clarifies and slightly tempers the original high-certainty claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Histone mark function, genomic distance, and cellular states collectively influence a histone mark’s relationship with transcription.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Histone mark function, genomic distance, and cellular states collectively influence a histone mark's relationship with transcription.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result verbatim; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: No individual histone mark is consistently the strongest predictor of gene expression across all genomic and cellular contexts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: No individual histone mark is consistently the strongest predictor of gene expression across all genomic and cellular contexts.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in preprint and publication; meaning unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study highlights the need to consider histone mark function, genomic distance, and cellular states when determining the effect of histone mark activity on transcriptional state.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study highlights the need to consider histone mark function, genomic distance, and cellular states when determining the effect of histone mark activity on transcriptional state.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is the same in both versions with no substantive changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In silico histone mark perturbation assays uncovered functional and disease-related loci.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In silico histone mark perturbation assays uncovered functional and disease related loci.
alignments.rationale: The result is carried over unchanged from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides frameworks for the use of chromatin deep learning models to uncover new biological insight.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides frameworks for the use of chromatin deep learning models to uncover new biological insight.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The virtual staining of virus-infected cells remains largely uncharted.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The virtual staining of virus-infected cells remains largely uncharted.
alignments.rationale: The limitation claim is identical in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a benchmark and datasets to address the detection of virus-infected cells using virtual staining.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a benchmark and datasets to address the detection of virus-infected cells using virtual staining.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim about proposing benchmark and datasets is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our work provides a comprehensive benchmark for the virus infection reporter virtual staining (VIRVS) task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our work provides a comprehensive benchmark for the virus infection reporter virtual staining (VIRVS) task.
alignments.rationale: The result about providing a comprehensive benchmark is carried forward verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We explore the VIRVS task employing U-Net and pix2pix architectures as prototypical regressive and generative models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We explore the VIRVS task employing U-Net and pix2pix architectures as prototypical regressive and generative models.
alignments.rationale: The methodological exploration is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model discovery in SINDy with hybrid dynamical systems outperforms alternative approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model discovery using hybrid dynamical systems outperforms alternative approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording is effectively identical and the result/strength is preserved between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is possible to infer models correctly up to high levels of biological noise of different types. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: It is possible to infer models correctly up to high levels of biological noise of different types. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is the same in content and asserted certainty in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework provides a practical approach for model discovery in biology when data are noisy and sparse. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach provides a practical framework for model discovery in biology when data are noisy and sparse. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same conclusion with only minor wording reorder; scope and confidence are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural networks are used to approximate the unknown dynamics of a system, enabling the denoising of data while learning the latent dynamics. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural networks are used to approximate the unknown dynamics of a system, enabling the denoising of the data while simultaneously learning the latent dynamics. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication adds the word "simultaneously," clarifying the joint nature of denoising and dynamics learning without changing scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel architecture UMINT produces a robust model for the integration of high-dimensional multi-omics data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: UMINT serves as a promising model for integrating variable number of single cell omics layers with high dimensions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication reframes "robust" as "promising" and emphasizes variable-number single-cell layers; this changes tone and slightly tempers the asserted robustness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: UMINT provides substantial reduction in the number of parameters compared to existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The parameter-reduction claim does not appear in the publication claims and so was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: UMINT is capable of learning a latent low-dimensional embedding that captures useful data characteristics. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model is capable of learning a latent low-dimensional embedding that can extract useful features from the data facilitating further downstream analyses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Content is the same (latent embedding extracting useful features) but the publication expresses higher confidence in the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of UMINT has been evaluated on benchmark CITE-seq datasets and it has outperformed existing state-of-the-art methods for multi-omics integration. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: UMINT has been benchmarked against existing state-of-the-art methods for single cell multi-omics integration. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication changes the strong performance claim ("outperformed") to a more neutral "benchmarked against" statement and lowers the asserted certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed regression method for predicting the melting temperature (Tm) of proteins uses 17,312 non-redundant proteins. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We developed a regression method for predicting the melting temperature (Tm) of proteins using 17,312 non-redundant proteins. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same methodological contribution and dataset size; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best model using ProtBert embeddings achieved a maximum correlation of 0.89 with an R² of 0.80 on the validation dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our best model, developed using ProtBert embeddings, achieved a maximum correlation of 0.89 with an R2 of 0.80 on the validation dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical numerical performance reported in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides a user-friendly web server and a python package for predicting and designing thermostable proteins. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our standalone software can be used to screen thermostable proteins at the genome level. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication reframes the provided tools as standalone software and emphasizes genome-level screening, broadening application scope while presenting slightly lower asserted certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of the developed method, PPTstab, successfully identifies thermostable proteins in different organisms from their genomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrated the application of PPTstab in identifying thermostable proteins in different organisms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The application/result claim is preserved between preprint and publication with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PromoGen is a collection of nucleotide language models that generates species-specific functional promoters efficiently across dozens of species. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PromoGen is a collection of nucleotide language models that generates species-specific functional promoters efficiently across dozens of species. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The artificial PromoGen-generated promoters (PGPs) for Escherichia coli and Bacillus subtilis demonstrated all distribution patterns of native promoters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Escherichia coli- and Bacillus subtilis-specific artificial PromoGen-generated promoters (PGPs) hold all distribution patterns of native promoters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about distribution patterns is preserved with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The overall score of PGPs is higher than that of promoters generated by another competitive neural network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The overall score of PromoGen-generated promoters is higher than that of promoters generated by another competitive neural network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same comparative performance claim retained in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental characterization showed that four of the tested B. subtilis PGPs reached the strong promoter level while all were active. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Four of the twenty-two experimentally characterized Bacillus subtilis PGPs reached the strong promoter level while all were active. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication adds the specific number (22) of experimentally characterized PGPs, clarifying the experimental context without changing the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work provides a valuable toolbox for the metabolic engineering of understudied microorganisms through efficient deep-learning strategies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work provides a valuable promoter toolbox for the metabolic engineering of understudied microorganisms. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion content is effectively the same; publication wording is slightly more concise but retains meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Abnormal infant General Movements (GMs) are a strong predictor of progressive neurodevelopmental disorders, including cerebral palsy (CP). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Abnormal infant General Movements (GMs) are a strong predictor of progressive neurodevelopmental disorders, including cerebral palsy (CP). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical conclusion retained between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep neural network model Efficientnet-b6 achieved an overall performance of 95.52% in tracking infant movements across all anatomical locations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep neural network model Efficientnet-b6 achieved an overall performance of 95.52% for GM tracking across all infants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication alters the phrasing of what "across all" refers to (from anatomical locations to infants), which clarifies the context though it shifts emphasis; the numeric performance is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed markerless pose-estimation scheme can track infant movements using consumer mobile device video recordings. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed markerless pose-estimation scheme can track infant movements using consumer mobile device video recordings. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is the same but the publication asserts higher certainty for this capability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An automatic, unsupervised strategy for performance evaluation on extensive out-of-domain recordings was introduced. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study introduces an automatic, unsupervised strategy for performance evaluation on extensive out-of-domain recordings. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The claim about the evaluation strategy is preserved verbatim with the same asserted certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings suggest the establishment of an automated GM tracking platform as a suitable alternative to current observational protocols for early diagnosis of neurodevelopmental disorders. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings suggest the potential for an automated GM tracking platform to support early diagnosis of neurodevelopmental disorders. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication changes the claim from being a "suitable alternative" to having "potential to support" diagnosis, which revises and somewhat narrows the asserted role; the stated certainty level is higher in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implementation of content-aware frame interpolation (CAFI) deep learning networks improves the temporal resolution of image series in microscopy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implementation of two content-aware frame interpolation deep learning networks improves the temporal resolution of image series post-acquisition.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result but specifies 'two' networks and clarifies it is post-acquisition processing, adding detail rather than changing the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CAFI predictions outperform standard interpolation methods by understanding the motion context of biological structures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CAFI is capable of understanding the motion context of biological structures and outperforms standard interpolation methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CAFI allows for reduced light exposure and phototoxicity on samples, enabling long-term live-cell imaging.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CAFI potentially allows for reduced light exposure and phototoxicity on the sample, enhancing long-term live-cell imaging.
alignments.rationale: The publication tones the claim from a definitive enabling statement to a potential benefit, weakening confidence while keeping the same scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of CAFI was benchmarked on six different datasets from three different microscopy modalities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of CAFI was benchmarked on 12 different datasets from four different microscopy modalities.
alignments.rationale: The publication changes the reported benchmarking results to more datasets and modalities and states the result with higher certainty, expanding scope and strengthening the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both DAIN and ZS, along with the training and testing data, are made available for the wider community via the ZeroCostDL4Mic platform.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report making these specific models and data available on ZeroCostDL4Mic, so the claim from the preprint is absent in the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep regularised models significantly outperform shallow CNNs and k-mer methods in the discovery of tissue-specific sites bound in vivo.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep regularized models significantly outperform shallow CNNs as well as k-mer methods in the discovery of tissue-specific sites bound in vivo.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this comparative performance claim with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed models can accurately predict HOXA2 co-binding sites based on MEIS differential binding across tissues.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis of ChIP-seq data from MEIS and HOXA2 allows for the accurate prediction of HOXA2 co-binding sites.
alignments.rationale: Both statements describe the same result (accurate prediction of HOXA2 co-binding sites) using the same datasets and target, with comparable certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Transfer-like and multitask approaches improve the performance of high-dimensional classification tasks in the context of TF binding analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Transfer-like and multitask approaches improve the regularization of high-dimensional classification tasks in the context of transcription factor binding.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes improvement in regularization (a more specific mechanism) rather than the broader 'performance' phrasing used in the preprint, clarifying the nature of the benefit.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Convolutional neural network (CNN) models can be applied to discover sequence features determining cooperative and differential transcription factor binding across tissues.
alignments.rationale: This added publication-level framing generalizes the contribution (application of CNNs to discover sequence features) beyond the specific preprint claims and was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automated approach using ChatGPT-4 resulted in a 9-fold increase in protein yields for colicin M and a 3-fold increase for colicin E1 compared to standard buffer compositions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implementation of the pipeline resulted in a 2- to 9-fold increase in yield of colicin M and E1 in both Escherichia coli and HeLa-based CFPS systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication modifies the reported yields (giving a 2–9x range for both proteins) and expands the systems tested (E. coli and HeLa), changing the quantitative claim and broadening scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of artificial intelligence and active learning in the CFPS systems demonstrates the potential to accelerate the optimization of complex biological processes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This framework enables reliable, automated workflows for routine synthetic biology.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the preprint's 'potential' claim into an enabling statement about reliable automated workflows, increasing assertiveness of the conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identifies limitations such as variability in CFPS and suggests future improvements in automation and reproducibility.
alignments.rationale: Explicit discussion of these specific limitations and suggested improvements is not represented among the publication claims provided, so the preprint limitation claim appears omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A fully automated Design-Build-Test-Learn (DBTL) pipeline was developed to streamline the optimization process of cell-free protein synthesis.
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts development of a DBTL pipeline, a framing not present in the preprint claims, so this is an added contribution-level claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Design phase of the pipeline was created entirely using ChatGPT-4, which dramatically reduced coding time.
alignments.rationale: The specific claim about ChatGPT-4 constructing the Design phase and reducing coding time is new in the publication and not stated in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The pipeline follows the Findable-Accessible-Interoperable-Reusable (FAIR) principles.
alignments.rationale: Adherence to FAIR principles is an added statement in the publication that does not have a counterpart in the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Pro-PRIME model successfully improved the alkali resistance of a VHH antibody capable of binding to growth hormone.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Pro-PRIME model successfully improved the alkali resistance of a VHH antibody capable of binding to growth hormone.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same experimental result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The selected mutant of the VHH antibody demonstrated enhanced functionality, including higher thermal stability, extreme pH resistance, and stronger affinity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The selected mutant of the antibody demonstrated enhanced functionality, including higher thermal stability, extreme pH resistance, and stronger affinity.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the functional enhancements of the selected mutant with the same details and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study validates the generalized capability of the LLM in meeting specific demands for protein design.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study validates the generalized capability of the LLM in meeting specific demands for protein design.
alignments.rationale: Both documents present the same conclusion about the LLM's generalized protein design capability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This is the first LLM-designed protein product successfully applied in mass production.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This is the first LLM-designed protein product successfully applied in mass production.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same novelty/contribution claim with equal certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An open-source, docker-based, and GPU-enabled Jupyterlab notebook infrastructure has been developed for rapid prototyping and developing end-to-end AI projects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An open-source, docker-based, and GPU-enabled JupyterLab infrastructure has been developed that runs on the public compute infrastructure of Galaxy Europe.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the target compute infrastructure (Galaxy Europe) while preserving the same contribution claim about the JupyterLab infrastructure.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed Jupyterlab notebook allows for the execution of long-running AI model training programs remotely on Galaxy's public compute infrastructure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: JupyterLab in Galaxy Europe is highly suitable for creating and managing AI projects.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames suitability for AI projects more generally; this aligns with the preprint's specific claim about executing long-running training on Galaxy, broadening the contextualization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The notebook supports GPU for faster training, git integration for version control, and the creation and execution of pipelines of notebooks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An open-source, docker-based, and GPU-enabled JupyterLab infrastructure has been developed that runs on the public compute infrastructure of Galaxy Europe.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the GPU-enabled, docker-based infrastructure; the preprint's additional feature details align as clarifications of the published infrastructure.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A recent scientific publication predicting infected regions of COVID-19 CT scan images was reproduced using multiple features of this notebook.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A recent scientific publication predicting infected regions in COVID-19 computed tomography scan images was reproduced using various features of JupyterLab on Galaxy Europe.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same reproduction result with equivalent detail and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Colabfold, a faster implementation of alphafold2, can be accessed in this notebook to predict the 3D structure of protein sequences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ColabFold, a faster implementation of AlphaFold2, can be accessed in JupyterLab to predict the 3-dimensional structure of protein sequences.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same functionality claim about ColabFold access and structure prediction capability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep Feature Interaction Maps (DFIM) accurately identifies ground truth motif interactions embedded in simulated regulatory DNA sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep Feature Interaction Maps (DFIM) accurately identifies ground truth motif interactions embedded in simulated regulatory DNA sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result claim word-for-word about DFIM performance on simulated sequences, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DFIM identifies synergistic interactions between GATA1 and TAL1 motifs from in vivo TF binding models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DFIM identifies synergistic interactions between GATA1 and TAL1 motifs from in vivo TF binding models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the identical claim about the GATA1–TAL1 interaction from in vivo models, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DFIM reveals epistatic interactions involving nucleotides flanking the core motif of the Cbf1 TF in yeast from in vitro TF binding models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DFIM reveals epistatic interactions involving nucleotides flanking the core motif of the Cbf1 TF in yeast from in vitro TF binding models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same in vitro Cbf1 flanking-nucleotide epistasis result, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DFIM reveals interactions between regulatory genetic variants and proximal motifs of target TFs as validated by TF binding quantitative trait loci. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DFIM reveals interactions between regulatory genetic variants and proximal motifs of target TFs as validated by TF binding quantitative trait loci. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same claim about variant–motif interactions validated by TF-binding QTLs, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach makes significant strides in improving the interpretability of deep learning models for genomics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding statement about broadly improving interpretability appears in the preprint but is not present among the publication claims, so it was removed from the published claim list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current surveillance strategies for tracking antimicrobial resistance have high false-negative error rates due to a lack of reference sequences with a representative coverage of AMR protein diversity. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: This limitation about surveillance false-negatives and reference sequence coverage is stated in the preprint but does not appear among the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A convolutional neural network was trained to differentiate antimicrobial resistance proteins from nonresistance proteins and functionally annotate them in nine resistance classes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An open-source bioinformatic tool was built to annotate antimicrobial resistance proteins and provide information on protein domains without sequence alignment. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasized training a CNN to classify/annotate into nine classes; the publication instead frames the outcome as an open-source annotation tool without explicitly restating the CNN training or the nine-class claim, so the contribution is reframed/narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model demonstrated higher recall values (>0.9) than the alignment-based approach for all protein classes tested. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The convolutional neural network (CNN) demonstrated higher recall values (> 0.9) than the alignment-based approach for all protein classes tested. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the quantitative performance claim (recall >0.9) for all tested classes, matching the preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our CNN architecture allowed us to investigate internal states and explain the model classification regarding protein domain feature importance related to antimicrobial molecule inactivation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CNN architecture allowed for the investigation of internal states and explanation of model classification regarding protein domain feature importance related to antimicrobial molecule inactivation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same interpretability/architecture claim about investigating internal states and domain feature importance, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We built an open-source bioinformatic tool that can be used to annotate antimicrobial resistance proteins and provide information on protein domains without sequence alignment. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An open-source bioinformatic tool was built to annotate antimicrobial resistance proteins and provide information on protein domains without sequence alignment. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the claim about providing an open-source annotation tool, matching the preprint contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients with rapid relapse triple-negative breast cancer (rrTNBC) are diagnosed at a higher stage compared to those with late relapse triple-negative breast cancer (lrTNBC). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific claim about diagnostic stage differences between rrTNBC and lrTNBC is present in the preprint but does not appear among the publication's listed claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Five immune signatures were significantly higher in no relapse triple-negative breast cancers (nrTNBCs) while lrTNBCs were enriched for eight estrogen/luminal signatures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both rapid relapse TNBC (rrTNBC) and late relapse TNBC (lrTNBC) have significantly lower immune signatures compared to no relapse TNBC (nrTNBC). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the immune-signature result at group level (rr and lr both lower than nr), which is consistent with the preprint's observation that nrTNBC has higher immune signatures; thus the claim is clarified/aggregated.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TP53 mutations are significantly more frequent in rrTNBC compared to lrTNBC. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The TP53-specific frequency difference is asserted in the preprint but is not reflected in the publication claims provided.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new approach to define triple-negative breast cancers based on timing of relapse has been developed. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication claims do not state development of a new TNBC-definition approach based on relapse timing, so this contribution claim was not retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Distinct clinical and genomic features can be incorporated into machine learning models to predict rapid relapse of triple-negative breast cancer. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Logistic regression models that incorporate genomic features significantly outperformed standard clinical/subtype models in training, testing, and independent validation cohorts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies that logistic regression models with genomic features outperformed clinical models across cohorts, which clarifies and strengthens the preprint's claim about using genomic features in predictive models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The secondary structure models of 1098 pri-miRNAs and 1456 human mRNA regions were determined through chemical probing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We present the secondary structure models of 1098 primary microRNAs and 1456 human messenger RNA regions determined through chemical probing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same dataset and chemical probing result, matching the preprint claim directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel deep learning architecture, eFold, was trained on a newly generated database and over 300,000 secondary structures. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We develop a deep learning architecture inspired from the Evoformer model of Alphafold and traditional architectures for secondary structure prediction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication describes the architecture's inspiration and development (Evoformer/Alphafold), which clarifies and reframes the preprint's description of the novel eFold architecture.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Benchmarking of eFold on two new test sets shows that the dataset and new architecture contribute to increasing prediction performance compared to state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our dataset and architecture contribute to increasing the prediction performance, compared to similar state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the benchmarking result that the dataset and architecture improve prediction performance versus state-of-the-art methods, matching the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Expanding the database size alone is insufficient for generalization across families of RNA structures. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Merely expanding the database size is insufficient for generalization across families. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same conclusion that expanding dataset size alone does not ensure generalization across families, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating a greater diversity and complexity of RNA structures allows for enhanced model performance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating a greater diversity and complexity of RNA structures allows for enhanced model performance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about diversity/complexity improving performance is preserved; the publication expresses it with higher certainty, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning algorithms can predict biological brain age from MRIs of healthy individuals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning algorithms can be trained to estimate age from brain structural MRI. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's phrasing to 'estimate age from brain MRI,' which clarifies but does not materially change the claim about ML-derived brain-age estimates.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predicted age difference (PAD) is a promising phenotype for studying normal brain aging and brain diseases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The interpretive claim about PAD as a promising phenotype appears in the preprint but is not present among the publication claims provided, so it was not carried forward.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A genome-wide association study (GWAS) of PAD identified two significant sequence variants, rs1452628-T and rs2435204-G. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A genome-wide association study (GWAS) of predicted age difference (PAD) in the UK Biobank data identified two sequence variants, rs1452628-T and rs2435204-G. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the GWAS identification of the same two variants in the UK Biobank, matching the preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The variant rs1452628-T is associated with reduced sulcal width, while rs2435204-G correlates with reduced white matter surface area. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The variant rs1452628-T is near KCNK2 and correlates with reduced sulcal width. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the rs1452628-T–sulcal width association and adds genomic context (near KCNK2); this clarifies that portion of the preprint claim. (The rs2435204-G part maps to the next publication claim.)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The genetic association analysis revealed three additional sequence variants associated with PAD. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The variant rs2435204-G correlates with reduced white matter surface area and tags a well-known inversion at 17q21.31. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports specific characterization of rs2435204-G but does not reiterate the preprint's broader statement about three additional associated variants; the claim is therefore narrowed/revised to highlight a specific variant's properties.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Deep Unsupervised Single-cell Clustering (DUSC) method integrates feature generation based on a deep learning architecture with a model-based clustering algorithm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method includes a technique to estimate an efficient number of latent features in the deep learning model.
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on a specific technical contribution (estimating latent features) that clarifies/operationalizes the preprint's broader statement about deep-learning-based feature generation; this narrows the original, more general claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DUSC generates robust clusters from single-cell transcriptomic data by providing a compact and informative representation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep unsupervised single-cell clustering (DUSC) method outperforms both classical and state-of-the-art feature learning and clustering methods.
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasizes representation and robustness of clusters, while the publication frames the outcome as outperforming other methods; this shifts emphasis from representation to comparative performance (a revision rather than exact match).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DUSC method outperforms both classical and state-of-the-art feature learning and clustering methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep unsupervised single-cell clustering (DUSC) method outperforms both classical and state-of-the-art feature learning and clustering methods.
alignments.rationale: The claim is restated essentially verbatim in the publication with consistent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method approaches the accuracy of supervised learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DUSC approaches the accuracy of supervised learning in analyzing single-cell transcriptomic data.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim but with higher stated certainty and domain specificity, increasing confidence in the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DUSC is freely available to the community and aims to improve understanding of the cellular atlas and enhance patient diagnostics and treatment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DUSC method aims to improve patient diagnostics and treatment by enhancing the understanding of the cellular atlas of living organisms.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the goal-oriented conclusion about improving diagnostics and understanding of the cellular atlas with similar wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The refined variant of ToxinPred demonstrates improved reliability and accuracy in predicting peptide toxicity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The refined variant of ToxinPred demonstrates improved reliability and accuracy in predicting peptide toxicity.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A deep learning model (ANN – LSTM with fixed sequence length) achieved a 0.93 AUROC and 0.71 MCC on independent data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning model (ANN - LSTM) achieved a maximum AUROC of 0.93 with MCC of 0.71 on an independent dataset.
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance metrics and model description are preserved exactly between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning model (extra tree) developed using compositional features of peptides achieved 0.95 AUROC and 0.78 MCC.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning model (extra tree) achieved a maximum AUROC of 0.95 with MCC of 0.78.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same model performance metrics and description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hybrid approaches, including motif-based and machine learning, achieved a 0.98 AUROC and 0.81 MCC.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hybrid method combining a motif-based approach with a machine learning-based model achieved a maximum AUROC of 0.98 with MCC 0.81.
alignments.rationale: The hybrid-method performance claim is carried over verbatim into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The evaluation on independent data demonstrates the superiority of the proposed method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method exhibited better performance than existing methods when evaluated on an independent dataset.
alignments.rationale: Both versions state the same comparative evaluation outcome on independent data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of a camouflage animal detection transfer learning step enhances the neural network’s tumor detection ability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The training strategy involving camouflage animal detection transfer learning improves neural network classification accuracy for brain cancer imaging data.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the central methodological innovation and its effect on tumor/brain cancer classification accuracy directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Training on glioma and normal brain MRI data demonstrates the potential success of the proposed training strategy for improving neural network classification accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The training strategy involving camouflage animal detection transfer learning improves neural network classification accuracy for brain cancer imaging data.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the demonstration as concrete improvement in classification accuracy, clarifying the preprint's more general statement about potential success on glioma/normal MRI data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Qualitative metrics indicate improved generalization ability by the models following camouflage animal transfer learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Qualitative metrics indicate that models exhibit improved generalization ability following camouflage animal transfer learning.
alignments.rationale: The qualitative-generalization claim is repeated in the publication with comparable wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The networks exhibit a high sensitivity to subtle structural changes resulting from the presence of a tumor.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Image saliency maps reveal that networks consider both the tumor and the surrounding tissue when making decisions.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's general statement about sensitivity is replaced by publication evidence (saliency maps) describing what the networks attend to; this revises the nature of the evidence and claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach to brain tumor MRIs is comparatively similar to that of trained radiologists.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach to brain tumor MRIs using neural networks is comparable to that of trained radiologists.
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts a comparable outcome with somewhat higher reported certainty (medium vs low), amplifying confidence in the original comparative claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning (DL) methods can address almost all aspects of microbiome analysis, including novel pathogen detection, sequence classification, patient stratification, and disease prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DL-based methods can address almost all aspects of microbiome analysis, including novel pathogen detection, sequence classification, patient stratification and disease prediction.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this broad capability claim for DL in microbiome analysis verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DL-based techniques significantly enhance the field of metagenomic analysis, paving the way for more accurate data analysis and better patient care.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning (DL) enables novel and promising approaches that complement state-of-the-art microbiome pipelines.
alignments.rationale: The publication tones down the preprint's stronger language about significantly enhancing patient care to a more cautious statement that DL complements existing pipelines, narrowing and tempering the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Analyzing metagenomic data remains challenging due to factors such as reference catalogs, sparsity, and compositionality.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include this explicit statement of challenges; instead it focuses on opportunities and interpretability, so this limitation claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The gut microbiome plays a crucial role in human health, providing vital information for patient diagnosis and prognosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The gut microbiome plays a crucial role in human health, providing vital information for patient diagnosis and prognosis.
alignments.rationale: Both documents state this conclusion identically, preserving scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Spatial transcriptomics technologies have the potential to profile gene expression at a single-cell resolution while maintaining cellular compositions within a tissue.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Spatial transcriptomics technologies have the potential to profile gene expression at a single-cell resolution while maintaining cellular compositions within a tissue.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this potential application of spatial transcriptomics with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Many existing spatial transcriptomics-specific methods are built upon traditional statistical or machine learning frameworks, which have shown to be sub-optimal in many applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many existing spatial transcriptomics-specific methods are built upon traditional statistical or machine learning frameworks, which have shown to be sub-optimal in many applications.
alignments.rationale: The critical assessment of existing methods is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning-based models have been developed to address the challenges specific to spatial transcriptomics analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning-based models have been developed to address the challenges specific to spatial transcriptomics analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this contribution claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning models for spatial transcriptomics analysis are nascent and remain largely underexplored.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning models for spatial transcriptomics analysis are nascent and remain largely underexplored.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this limitation remark with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The review discusses new frontiers and open questions in the field of spatial transcriptomics, highlighting potential transformational applications of deep learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The review discusses new frontiers and open questions in the field of spatial transcriptomics, highlighting potential transformational applications of deep learning.
alignments.rationale: The review's scope and concluding outlook are preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepRetroMoCo improves motion correction procedures in fMRI data acquired from the cervical spinal cord. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepRetroMoCo represents a substantial improvement in motion correction procedures for fMRI data acquired from the cervical spinal cord. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same overall conclusion but in stronger, more certain language ('substantial improvement' and higher reported certainty), so the claim is clarified and its confidence amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The average tSNR in the cervical cord was significantly higher when DeepRetroMoCo was used for motion correction compared to the sct_fmri_moco method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepRetroMoCo significantly improves the average temporal signal-to-noise ratio (tSNR) in the cervical cord compared to the sct_fmri_moco method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same statistically significant tSNR improvement with equivalent emphasis and certainty; wording is equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Average DVARS was lower in images corrected by DeepRetroMoCo than those corrected by sct_fmri_moco. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The average DVARS values are lower in images corrected by DeepRetroMoCo, indicating a superior reduction in motion artifacts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same DVARS reduction but adds an explicit interpretation (superior reduction in motion artifacts), so the result is clarified without changing scope or reported confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The average processing time for DeepRetroMoCo was significantly shorter than for sct_fmri_moco. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepRetroMoCo exhibits a significantly shorter processing time compared to sct_fmri_moco. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same measured result about processing time with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both RNN and SepFormer models significantly improved CI listener’s speech intelligibility in noise without compromising the perceived quality of speech. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both RNN and SepFormer algorithms significantly improved CI listener’s speech intelligibility in noise without compromising the perceived quality of speech overall. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same experimental result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models increased the intelligibility in stationary non-speech noise and introduced substantially more improvements in non-stationary speech noise. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DNN algorithms increased intelligibility in stationary non-speech noise and introduced a substantial improvement in non-stationary noise. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same pattern of results for stationary and non-stationary noise; wording differences are minor and do not change meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Conventional signal processing strategies provide little benefits in non-stationary speech noise. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current single-microphone noise reduction algorithms in hearing aids and CIs are ineffective against realistic, non-stationary noise such as multi-talker interference. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates a similar concern but reframes it more strongly ("ineffective" vs "little benefits") and narrows the focus to single-microphone algorithms in hearing aids/CIs, so the claim is revised and narrowed with amplified language.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DNN models show promise as a solution for listening challenges in multi-talker noise interference. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not restate the general conclusion that DNN models broadly 'show promise' for multi-talker interference; instead it focuses on specific results and other conclusions, so this broader conclusive claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed DeepInsight-3D approach can predict patient-specific anticancer drug response from three types of multiomics data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed DeepInsight-3D approach can predict patient-specific anticancer drug response from three types of multi-omics data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same core claim about predicting drug response from three omics types with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepInsight-3D outperformed two other state-of-the-art methods for predicting drug response. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepInsight-3D outperformed other state-of-the-art methods applied to the task of predicting drug response. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions report that DeepInsight-3D outperformed state-of-the-art methods; the publication omits the exact count of comparators but conveys the same result and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The structured data-to-image conversion in DeepInsight-3D allows for effective use of convolutional neural networks to handle high dimensionality. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly state this particular framing about handling high dimensionality via structured data-to-image conversion, so this specific claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of additional channels in DeepInsight-3D can accommodate data from different ‘omics layers while encoding their connections. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method effectively uses additional channels of an image to accommodate data from different omics layers while encoding their connections. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same technical claim but states it more assertively (higher certainty and framed as a result), so content is unchanged while confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These advances can facilitate the development of better personalized treatment strategies for different cancers in the future. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed improvements can facilitate the development of better personalized treatment strategies for different cancers in the future. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same forward-looking conclusion but expresses it with increased certainty, so the claim is unchanged in scope but its confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A workflow for the design of a small, energy-efficient recurrent convolutional neural network (RCNN) architecture for AFib detection is proposed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A workflow for the design of a small, energy-efficient recurrent convolutional neural network (RCNN) architecture for AFib detection is proposed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model consists of a small segmented CNN in combination with an optimal energy classifier, utilizing only 114 trainable parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model consists of a small segmented CNN with only 114 trainable parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same model description and numeric parameter count; differences in phrasing are negligible.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The architectural decisions in the model are made by considering energy consumption as a metric equally important as accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The architectural decisions in the model are made by using energy consumption as a metric equally important as accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same methodological emphasis on energy consumption alongside accuracy with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The comparison with previous studies suggests that widely used huge CNNs for similar tasks are mostly redundant and computationally expensive. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The comparison with previous studies suggests that widely used huge CNNs for similar tasks are mostly redundant and computationally expensive. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same comparative conclusion about large CNNs being redundant and costly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduced proteome-scale feature discovery approach for intrinsically disordered regions (IDRs) is called 'reverse homology'. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduced proteome-scale feature discovery approach for intrinsically disordered regions (IDRs) is called 'reverse homology'. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution name and framing without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reverse homology approach allows the neural network to learn conserved features of IDRs that can be interpreted as motifs, repeats, or bulk features. (certainty=high, type=result)
